text:
Absolutely horrible experience all around from long waits with customer service, being told conflicting information, and rigid inconvenient guidelines.

I purchased a hotspot to use during a hospital rotation in Arizona. I was told I would have LTE data to complete school assignments, etc. Because of the location and poor communication, I was roaming constantly with only 1 MB of data speed. We wanted to have the issue resolved. My husband had work with customer care over the course of 12+ times. Each time we were given conflicting information stating that it should be faster but alas it still was slower than 2G. At times I was not even able to send an iMessage on the phone. 
We chose to return the device however I was 2 hr one way drive away. We were told the return date was firm at 14 days and even though I work 12hr shifts at the hospital I would have to return it on time and drive 4 hr total. Multiple customer care agents told us we could send it in via mail return but then those options were revoked at the last minute without explanation. 

Once I drove the 2hr one way, the first store was unable to process the return due to their app not working. We drove to a second store, same issue. We were told we would have to drive to the third store but they said “no promises it will work.”
We had the second store call customer service and proceeded to be on hold 30min on two separate occasions.

This whole experience has been absolutely exhausting and incredibly disappointing. We hope to get a refund for our device, but that does not come close to the time driving, at the stores and on the phone that we have lost as a result of T-Mobile’s poor customer service. 
I am not one to write negative reviews; however, I wanted to make others aware to avoid their service because of what my husband and I experienced.

Jorge C. 13073 obt. 32837 October 6th gave me the individual  attention  that made me feel special. Set up my new phone, made me feel like the transfer of phones was smooth.. All apps photos contacts etc.saved! Thank you for restoring  my faith in t-mobile loyalty to 21 year customer. Jorge is a gem to your business  I hope you show him his value. Thank. You! Pamela Acosta

After being a tmobile customer for over ten years, I thought I'd take a chance on their internet. BIG effing mistake.

It never worked.  One bar service.  Huge disappointment. I was never able to use it.  Closed my account, sent back the equipment, and then got a call FROM A COLLECTION AGENCY (not even a notification from tmobile) saying that I owed them on an account that never worked, which was closed.  Tracked the equipment -- they have it.

I'm someone who checks my credit score all the time and have never in my life been sent to a collection agency for any reason.

I guess Tmobile is now owned by Satan and the forces of darkness.

I had an outstanding customer experience at the T-Mobile Store, 4225 Ramsey Street, Fayetteville, North Carolina! The Representative, Isaac, went "above and beyond" helping me get my new iPhone 13 Pro Max activated! I had problems getting the SIM card out of my old phone (iPhone 6S), and he literally provided me with the utmost professionalism, compassion and expertise! It took almost an hour to transfer my information to my new phone. He was not only patient through that process - but he went ABOVE and BEYOND by installing my new phone in the protective case and putting on the protective shield for me; both items I brought with me to the store, but had planned to do myself! I also watched Isaac treat other customers that entered into the store with that same level of warmth and professionalism! Thank you Isaac - and thank you T-Mobile!

Do yourself a favor and run away from them as fast as you can, either go to AT&T, Verizon for a true carrier experience, or go with a discount carrier to save money. Cause T mobile offers neither in their services. They play a bait and switch game with customers and promise to take care of issues just to keep the bills coming. And the only way to get any real answers from them is to make complaints with regulatory agencies in order to get a response from management.

edits.text:
T mobile honestly does not deserve 1 star. They are straight up built around false promises and leading customers on.

I started our service with them in March, the first week we noticed our service was spotty at our residence so I called and they said that we were covered by a towner being modernized and our service would improve. Fast forward 3 months and our service never improved, multiple conversations with customer service, tech support and even swapping out phones. Some longer than 2 hours. In the end I contacted customer support and canceled service and ported my number out of T mobile with the promise from t mobile that they would not be charging me anything further due to the trouble. Well 4 months later I get a credit delinquency notice because they never canceled my service. No phone call, email, bill or text. 

Stay away from them, stay far far away.

text: Customer experience is horrible

Thank you Mr Mario,his employee ID:1213201 for help me resolve the issue that I have with my t-mobile account. I really appreciate you sir. I have been dealing with this issue awhile after making a lot of calls to t-mobile costumer service .but today Mr Mario solved my issue in literally 5 minutes. I'm beyond grateful. That's why sir you deserve 10 stars .thank you sir for your srevice.

I must say that having Sprint acquired by T-Mobile has certainly made it difficult to get my phone serviced.  But, today, in Idaho Falls was an exception.  I was trying to send T-mobile my phone to get the screen repaired and the loaner phone that they sent to me just would not work despite spending two hours on the phone with their service people.  So, they directed me to a sprint/tmobile outlet in Idaho Falls in a shopping mall where I was lucky to encounter Emett. Emett used to be in the military until his eardrums were destroyed and he was returned to civilian life. Emett pointed out that T-mobile does not engage in repairs at their stores as Sprint did (bad change I must say), but he would do everything he could to try to get my loaner phone working.  And so as he ran between me and other customers he ultimately fixed my phone!  He was great and I'm sure would have been a Sprint superstar- too bad Sprint is disappearing.  But Emett did for me what Sprint would have done and I am so grateful!

I switched to T-mobile a few short months back. I switched due to their claims of their 5G coverage and improved reception and such.. All this is bull. Before switching, I wanted to make sure the area where I worked and lived. The map SHOWS total coverage of each in the supposed amazing 5G..However,  when I started using them, my coverage was anything but great. I hardly get any service at home, and little to no service at work. When I do have service, the signal sucks, data is almost as good as dial up, and is probably the worst cellular service I've seen in years. I want to switch, but was conned into 2 phones that must be paid for. AT&T does cover my area, and is actually cheaper. I'll be going to them as soon as I can get the phones paid down. Avoid T-mobile at all cost!

Absolute garbage all around.  Customer service is garbage.  Cell service is garbage.  I have Home Internet because I live in an area with poor selection and I was excited when TMob was available.  However, my experience has been nothing but garbage.

First the modems were back ordered by more than a month and I couldn't even sign up for an account until I got it.  While ordering the modem the service agent, Tyler, walked me through setting up auto pay.  

When I received the modem I activated and tried to log in but was unable to.  I didn't figure it mattered, the service was working and delivering 1/2 the speeds they told me I'd probably get but it was at least faster than what I had at the time,  I had autopay so they'd get their payments, etc.  I figured I'd deal with it eventually.

Two months later my speeds are horrible so I try rebooting the modem and it comes up and doesn't provide me internet service.  I check my email and find that they had deactivated my service due to non-payment.  It was 2am at this point so I called the next day.  They had a ONE AND A HALF HOUR wait on hold!!! 

I wasn't going to sit there for that so I tried to pay using the robot but it wouldn't tell me how much I owed them because it kept asking for a PIN (which I never set up).  Finally after enough button mashing and frustration the robot told me how much I owed and I promptly paid and figured I'd deal with the rest of the problems when I had more patience.

A week or so ago I called again to ask about the internet speed issues I've been experiencing.  The robot told me it'd be a 10 minute wait.  I waited for 35 minutes.  The whole time the stupid recording telling me that "T-Mobile customers are the happiest customers" and "Every customer gets a TEAM OF EXPERTS so you get a person FAST and you don't GET BOUNCED AROUND." which I heard at least 4 times while sitting on hold.  

Someone finally picked up and the first thing she said was "Oh you only have Home Internet?  I need to transfer you to another department." which I waited another 10 minutes for them to pick up....

They start asking me for a PIN.  Again, I never set up a PIN.  They tell me it's required and it's the "only way" and it's "only 6 digits" as if somehow that was supposed to help.  I told them I didn't know it and never set one up.  They told me the only way to verify my identity, which was required to even discuss my internet speed issues, was to drive to the nearest tmob store (1.5hrs away) with 2 forms of identification to identify myself.

I said "If it's going to be this difficult, cancel my service." they told me they couldn't do that without my PIN.  I said "I don't *have* to pay you so tell me where to send this modem and we'll just never hear from each other again" which they told me they couldn't because they needed to make an RMA to get that address and that only can happen with my PIN.

The person I was speaking to escalated me to their supervisor which did exactly the same thing, including offering to pay for my gas???, and eventually I had to get off of the phone for a meeting and told them I'd call back again.

I have, over the last few months of having this terrible company's poor excuse for a service, been trying to contact them on Twitter nonstop.  Most of the time I start with "I don't have a PIN or an account but everyone tells me I'm required to have a PIN" and they start with asking me to verify my PIN or log into my account.  

FINALLY, today, I spoke with someone named Lynnette who managed to help me get an account set up.  Something weird was configured on their end with my email address.  I could receive emails to it but when I entered it as my account email I got "Email address not recognized" errors.  So now I've got an account.  The first thing I did was checked my account configurations and BIG SHOCKER!!! I didn't have a PIN set!  :mind-blown: 

So now I've got an account and my PIN set but unfortunately (but also reasonably) Lynnette was at the end of her shift and had to go.  I do still have a major problem with my home internet speeds.  They're dreadful and worse than my DSL anymore.  They used to be decently fast, at least compared to my DSL, but now it's slow and sometimes completely unusable.  

While on the site I noticed my data usage and a little thing that said "Customers who use over 50GB/mo" could be throttled.  Which, I was straight up told, by Tyler, that there would be no throttling and that data was unlimited.  I asked it multiple times in multiple different ways because that's very important to me.  I use a lot of data and wouldn't have signed up for a garbage service that would throttle me because I use a lot of data.

Now I'm talking to customer service and they confirm that I could be throttled.  I don't think that's what's going on with my service though it's too consistent.  I just want this stupid company to deliver what I pay for.  It's ridiculous.

STAY AWAY FROM T-MOBILE.  THEY'RE A JOKE

I have had T-Mobile for almost 8 months and have had nothing but problems. I have a work phone so I always had a backup and it is a pain to switch which is why I kept it. Their customer service is horrible, the website never works and the cell service is the worst of all providers. I had issues for the first 5 months where text messages of mine would go to random numbers and I would get responses from them and only 35% of the calls ever go through and if someone else called me they would get random people to answer or the call wouldn't go through. There is still no resolution to this. I pay with prepaid and they would give different amounts each time I would call and when I gave them the call logs of when I called the tech support to tell them who I talked with last they would always say there was no record of it (3 times). They send the disconnect messages even if you have paid so no one really knows what is going on. Service is always 50/50. Finally, sick of fighting with them on problems and finally giving up and switching. Please do not use their service! It is Horrible!!!
Worst customer service. I waited on call for 30 min no one answered.

T-Mobile are thieves! they always try to charge extra fee even if you do everything as directed. I talked to an agent earlier to set a payment plan and she said there is not going to be any late fee! and NOW I have to pay a late fee even when my payments were on time! Liars and thieves!

Obviously, T-mobile doesn't read the reviews here or anywhere else, they don't care. I was with Verizon for 22 years until they got rid of their customer service completely. (Partly due to the i-phone as well). I then found T-mobile and got tricked into drinking the koolaid. For the first few months I was impressed with the service and the price, which was lower than I was paying with Verizon by $10 to $15! 
I have had a problem with making calls for two months and after trying for two weeks everyday to get in touch with T-mobile, I finally did. (The recording says that because of the i-phone, they are unable to take calls or there is a two to three hour wait. By the way, the call back feature they offer does nothing, you never get that call).
The tech was questionable and sounded strange to me. He was able to fix the situation, but would not tell me what the issue was. I asked several times and he kept repeating that he reset the phone from his end.
I have been looking for a service that does not offer the i-phone and finally found one, but the service cost is double what I pay with T-mobile, but at least they answer the phone even though they are also high pressure. I am not going with them because of that. I do not trust them that I won't be dropped once I sign up. 
I have a standard flip phone, that is all I need. I have no interest or need for a smart phone. I only need a real phone for emergencies and such.
I have been thinking about canceling T-mobile for a couple of weeks and just going without a cell phone. I decided to test that  and call them to discuss what I need to do and of course I can't reach anyone (according to the recording it is because of the i-phone). I found out the 60% of their customers go for that i-phone piece of crap. what that means is that T-mobile doesn't care about 40% of their customers, which they are about to lose. They also made a mistake buying Sprint as it has made them too big to care now. 
Then there is the constant lying on how they listen to customers and such. There is no evidence of that. It is clear they only listen to those customers buying the $1000 i-phone that will be dead in 3 months.

I'm now wondering if the wait at a T-mobile store to cancel my account will be days long as well?

I am a Sprint customer for several years never has a lick of issues until T Mobile took over.  Service sucks now the customer service sucks and no one in the stores can help.They tell you to call customer service and when you do the long hours of waiting start. I have logged 14 almost 15 hours of hold time waiting for them. Finally when you do get someone they transfer you it disconnects.  Went to the store in SOUTHAVEN MS and again got the run around told me call customer service we can not help you. Our systems are not compatible. Advise do not merge when you have no knowledge of they other company.   NEVER WOULD I RECOMMEND THEM TO ANYONE!

T-Mobile AND Sprint  - STAY AWAY!!!

Sprint was bad enough, but when T-Mobile bought them out, it only got inconceivably worse! I LITERALLY CANNOT WAIT for my contract to be up so I can switch to ANYONE besides the T-Mobile/Sprint conglomeration! I haven't been able to log into my account to view details for MONTHS!

I was so dissatisfied with their UTTERLY INEPT customer "service" reps, that I switched my phone to another carrier (Spectrum). I still have three other phones for my family on this account and I CAN'T LOG IN too see details including payment and contract info because they INSIST on sending me a text message to THEIR phones just to log in.

I've contacted their corporate office and they have tried UNSUCCESSFULLY to navigate their OWN system! They keep telling me to just contact them if I can't log in but it's 6:30am PST and nobody is around! I need to check acct details before work and how am I to contact them and WHY can't they fix this? They keep saying they fixed it and I can verify using email, but there is NEVER a prompt to verify via email, only my two son's accounts (one is in the Marines and the other lives 200 miles away) or my 83-year old uncle who doesn't text.

HOW am I supposed to verify my account????????????? YOU GUYS SUCK!

T-Mobile has constantly lied to me about the delivery of my iPhone. I pre-ordered an iPhone 13 pro max 1 TB on the earliest day that we could preorder (17 Sep 2021) for a delivery on 24 September 2021. Only “after” I ordered the phone I was sent an email stating that it was on back order. I am still waiting for the phone. When I called and inquired about when I was going to get my the phone, I was told 3 different answers-from the next day, to 3 business days, to soon. I do not understand why T-Mobile allows one to preorder yet when you do your phone is on back order. If that is the case, what is the purpose of preordering an iPhone? In addition, why do they not tell you while you are ordering the phone that your phone will be on back order. It’s as if no one ever heard of keeping track of an inventory. I have had T-Mobile for 15 years; brought at least 8 10 phones from them, always paid my bill on time, and yet I cannot get a preordered iPhone on the expected delivery day. I know there may be a chip shortage and if that is the case, they should have postpone delivering the phone like they did last year. I am seriously considering going to another cell phone provider.
They were horrible years ago and they still suck! Service is horrible, total waste
It’s DUCKING DOG CRUD SERVICE don’t be talked into anything. Paying up the butt for 5g service and I can’t even get anything too work at my house worst switch I have ever made

Omg-if I could do a 0 star review. I would. I had almost ZERO service for several months, always promising they're working on towers and then one would be fixed and another would go down. I contributed to be lied to and made false promises. I was then told 2 different times that I needed to speak to the higher technical department who promised a few hundred dollars in refunds because of the horrible service and overage charges because someone went in and changed my plan (to benefit the company financially of course). I leave in August and had to call and pay final bills of which now in October I'm told wasn't oaid in full. Along with the fact that since I'm no longer their customer, they cut off online access so they think they can bill me but no proof of what they're billing for. The lies are ridiculous! The lack of notes from the last set of lies isn't even in the system and then they offer to send me my previous statements for $5 each! This company is horrible! The service doesn't work, the customer service isn't trained well other than to feed lies and the accountability is not there at all. They never want to transfer to a supervisor after multiple requests and try to give you information you didn't even ask for to appear as if they're assisting. I will make sure I NEVER support, refer or do business with them ever again. I will make sure to share my horrific experience, unfair treatment - service wise as well as financially (only to benefit the company). This company is full of lies, scam and horrible customer service with a few sprinkles of nice people that unfortunately are verbally nice but still aren't actually able to get anything done for the customers sake.
T-Mobile sick ! Worse service everrrrr

Sprint customer here. Our phones were no longer compatible with the new services after sprint got bought over. Went to the tmobile store, sat there for 3 hours with two kids under the age of 4. They didn't have the phones we wanted in stocks, no big deal. The representative said they would mail us our new phones in 3 business days. He switched our sim cards and our phones wouldn't work at all. He said that we could still use social media and messaging through that. We don't have social media and I run a small business. Not having a working phone was not going to happen. He got ahold of the store manager and made it so we could still receive phone calls but no internet no email and texts were only received when I got home at night and was connected to wifi. Those "3 business days" came and went. Called tmobil and they were on backorder for another 3 weeks. Switched to Verizon, in and out with new phones in 45 minutes. What a f'ing joke. As I'm writing this I'm on the phone with tmobile to cancel the new phone shipment and the wait time is 1 hour 30 minutes. Also the price difference was about 20 bucks a month higher for Verizon unlimited with 3 lines.

I just signed up with T-Mobile. The offered a discount on an Apple Watch which I agreed to purchase. They did not tell me that to receive the discount price I must sign up for the watch cell plan. They also just added a protection plan for the iPhone without even asking me if I want it. Lastly I was told I could get an insider discount plan. After calling them back to confirm this I found out that I was not eligible for this. They must be paying staff an incentive for these additional items added to my account but am not sure. Either way it is unacceptable to simply add items to a new account without explanation.

T-Mobile shipped an unboxed iPhone13 Pro Max to me! When UPS delivered my order from T-Mobile in front of my door, I opened the package immediately. I found the iPhone box has been opened by someone since the seal on the iPhone box has been removed. I stopped the carrier man and rejected receiving the package. I took pics of the shipping package as well as the iPhone box. There is another address on the shipping package. The iPhone must have been opened by someone living at that address. I was frustrated. My order should be a brand new iPhone13 Pro Max, but the phone was used/refurbished/unboxed, at least it is not a brand-new iPhone13 Pro Max. I called T-Mobile many times so they can ship a brand-new device to me. After many calls, they asked me if I want to pick up a device in the store. I was excited and thought I could pick up a new phone today. I asked the representative when I could pick up a device, she said she was not sure since all stores do not have devices now. So why did she tell me to pick up a device in the store? A week ago, the same representative promised me that I could have my new phone by 10/05/2021 (the last date that T-Mobile promises when I can get a device in my order). Now I need to wait another month! I could have been happy to have a new phone but I am not now. I am exhausted to call T-Mobile again and again. Finally, the lady told me to wait until they have devices in stock and the date is 10/27/2021. Avoid T-Mobile to save your life and time. I have a serious headache due to my new phone order from T-Mobile. Note: Open your package immediately when you receive an iPhone order from T-Mobile, or it’s hard to explain that you received an unboxed iPhone from T-Mobile. Also, make sure you take pictures as evidence

I loved Sprint, never a problem. I have a broken phone so cannot log in because the stupid system only sends code to phone. Jerks had me wait over 45 minutes because the chat does not answer anything. I read all the comments on their facebook site, here, and all over. They have gone to Sh***! Thanks for all the reviews, I am going back to Cricket because I really liked their service years ago and they never try to bait and switch or send me a phone that costs 1000 !!
EDIT: Cricket was about as bad. What happened to people???  Half these CEOs should be fired.

I wish I could give 0 stars they have been so terrible!
I called 3 times today and was on the phone for over 3 hours trying to get an iphone 13 forever plan.

The first guy had  it ready to charge to my account then we were disconnected and he didn't call back. I was on an hour and fifteen minute wait. 

Once I got a call back the new associate was trying to charge for the phone. I asked to speak with a supervisor waited 45 more minutes just to be disconnected again.

Called back again and it's an hour and a half wait. I'm done and will tell everyone how awful this company is.

The reason for the new phone is my new Pixel 4a 5g doesn't send my text messages and I drop calls all of the time. We have 2 cellphone towers in my backyard!
I called they said they don't have 5g in my area. I live between Baltimore, Annapolis, and Washington DC. Why wouldn't they have 5g in that area? 

I will make sure to let everyone know how awful the customer service is and how horrific the cell service is. I'm cancelling my account and going back to Verizon, I'd rather pay a couple extra bucks to avoid the hassle.

This is the worst company in America -- if that's even where they're located -- for customer service. From the agents in the stores to their horrible phone contact to their come-on lying dishonest advertising to believing a single word anyone at this company ever tells you -- these folks stink to high heaven. Pay a few $$ more and go with an honest company

They had a promotion with the Galaxy A32, that if you trade in another phone, you'll get it for free. That's what we did and we've been getting charged every month since for the new phones that we should be getting for free. We've called them multiple times and they said they would fix it, they've emailed us confirming they received our old phones and that they would fix and yet, we're still having to call about the same issue that's been going on for months.
Do yourself a favor and find a carrier that cares about customer service
abysmal company. I will tell everyone I meet as long as I live to choose any one but tmobile.

Service is sh***yaf before sprint had this dumb collab there service wasn’t to bad but now it’s completely outta line. They claim to have 5G ?!? 5G my ass it’s slow af can’t even get calls half the time 10/10 don’t recommend.

Tmobile is fraud. Nothing is free. Don't believe the customer service representative. Most of them needs to be retrained. They are very clueless. They keep transferring you and still can't resolve nothing. Shame!

Me and my husband bought 2 lines from T-mobile and were offered a third line for free. Later we got the bill and realized it cost us $40. I called the customer service and asked them to cancel it and they said they were taking care of it. We received the second bill with an extra $40 on it. The same story!
I called customer service again and I heard the same thing! And today for the third time!!! OMG

I went to the tmobile on Dixie hwy, Louisville kentucky.  they didn't have the screen saver I needed and ask if they had anything close, he just laughed at me. he told me to go to Amazon or Walmart.  I went to a metro pcs store and found a screen saver that fit, even tho not the exact same Screensaver but it fit. the guy at metro took the time to find one that fit. I'm going to metro from now on

Very disappointed since Sprint changed…

Very disappointed since Sprint changed to T mobile. Service is bad and dead zones. As soon as my contract is up I'm switching to Verizon.
Very disappointed since Sprint changed to T mobile. Service is bad and dead zones. As soon as my contract is up I'm switching to Verizon.

We were with Sprint for 12 years. As soon as the linked up with Tmobile they went to s***! Our bill almost doubled and our service became garbage. So disappointed in such a huge company. Stay away from Sprint/Tmobile!

Hubs & I have been with T-Mobile for about 17-18years now. Our son has joined us on our phone plan & we now have the internet in our home provided by T-Mobile. We’ve had our battles in the past but I really cannot complain about their services. I had received a few emails from them this past couple weeks, asking to take a short survey & get a gift. I thought it was total bologna but did it anyway. Less than 2 weeks later, there was the prize that I had chosen in my mailbox!!!! Thank you T-Mobile!!! You are appreciated.

I have been with tmobile for 18 years and complained during that time about their poor service. Calls were being dropped, can't dial a number, can't hear the other party and a number of other technical issues. The last call I had with them finally made up my mind for me. The customer representative was extremely rude. I decided then to terminate my service with them after 18 years. Too bad, tmobile you may finally go out of business. You should consider providing excellent technical and customer service rather than trying to buy up all your competition.

T Mobile has been nothing but great...ok several areas up 75N towards Lee that's sketchy but that's it. Good customer service and can't beat the plans...a lot of entitled complaining here on this page...wah, wah, wah...

Be warned-I have never had so many problems, in so short a time, with any organization like T-Mobile. I have been on the phone for over 6 hours trying to get a promised refund and the rules change each and every time I call. 
I switched from Verizon for their poor service and find T-Mobile to be even worse.

Terrible customer service. They treat you like you’re just a number. My ex husband and I separated our phone lines. They changed his line to Magenta right away, but refused to change my plan until the next billing cycle. So, now I’m stuck paying $125 for two lines when I’ve only had one phone line for a month. They don’t care about the customer at all.

if you plan on getting a service do not go with this one. The agents lie to you and will pad your bill very quickly. Nothing absolutely nothing is free with this company. They also do not have the best customer service it took me a week to try to get somebody. I would recommend you guys go to AT&T or Verizon they'll treat their customers better. I'm stuck legally with their contract for Sprint. I cannot get out of this. But do not come this company they will lie to you every step of the way.

Went to the T Mobil Store at 10670 Culebra Road and W Loop 1604 in San Antonio and worked with Rachelle to correct the Billing on our cell phone service.  Rachelle listened to what had happened took our contract and went in the back office.  When she return we had good news.  The bill got straightened out to our satisfaction.  Rachelle is the Assistant Store Manager.  She knows how to treat people and knows what customer satisfaction means.  We are seniors ages 76 and 77 and so often are not treated with the Respect and Dignity that Rachelle showed to us.  In addition Rachelle reset our cloud names that were misspelled and downloaded the yahoo mail app so we could access mail on our cell phone.  Rachelle is a Breath of Fresh air, she is one of a kind and a Excellent T Mobil Employee.  She deserves a merit increase and if I had a business I would hire Rachelle in a blink of an eye, which my friends is faster than a New York Minute.  Thank you Rachelle for your Kindness and Patience with us to get our billing issue resolved.

I had sprint before for many years all good but when T Mobile took over I had no signal their customer service it's the worse so I decided to switch to another company and then on the last bill they charge me $245 for no reason I can explained I had no contract or had no new phones. I will not recommend T Mobile
Lamar was so PATIENT WONDERFUL AND SOME OF THIS BEST CUSTOMER SERVICE I HAVE EVER EXPERIENCED FIXED ALL MY PROBLEMS HE IS BEAUTIFUL INSIDE AND OUT SO VERY POLITE HALES CORNERS WISC

I switched to t mobile and I retreat it.  When I had cricket I received nicer phone for free never had any problems with internet or calls. T mobile I got worst phone for free having trouble with internet connection not clear on phone calls and pay twice as much. I still get the same ads when I had cricket for half price.  You do the math.

Charged me 30 dollars for a disputed charge of 1.17$ that was taken out more than a month after I had switched to another service. Then sent it to collections. 
After being a good customer for >6years they treated me that way. Choose another provider😡

T-mobile is a fraud and should be avoided at all cost. I was introduced to their bait and switch internet program. They offered me a "free" tablet and added a line that I never requested without my consent and knowledge. When I told them to cancel the line, they told me that I cannot return the tablet and have to pay full price value. Stay away from t-mobile at all cost. You've been warned.

BLUF: worst customer service. No protection of customer information. My phone number was outported to different carrier. Not only that, they added 2 new lines without my approval. Unbelievable! I am a victim of T mobile negligence. If you want a good network service and satisfactory customer service, t- mobile is NOT the company you want. Horrible business company with full of lies

So I've had many different cell services and I think my favorite is T-Mobile. So far their service (coverage and speed) have greatly surpassed any others I've had. They can be maybe a little more spotty when traveling by car, but that's nothing compared to the positive experience overall. Their customer service is no question MUCH better than Verizon's. Which really does have such a huge impact. I always end up switching back to them. Every single T-Mobile associate I've spoke with seems to genuinely care about people, in different stores and when I call customer service. EVEN when transferring out of T-Mobile's service. The prices are absolutely worth the service you recieve altogether, as long as it's like this I'm not ever switching again.

I want to remove takeover protection. They ask me to call them. I wait an hour in line and the support said their side remove it. However, I take a look website still did not remove. 
I hate tmobile customer service. They just waste my time. I will never ever use tmobile service again.

I switched cell phone companies recently from Sprint/T-Mobile to Verizon because Verizon has better coverage and my son‘s phone had not been paid off yet so I called Sprint, talk to a young man, and asked them why is my son‘s phone still locked, as the new SIM card from Verizon was not working.? They told me that they cannot unlock the phone until the phone is paid off. I immediately went to the bank and made a deposit of the amount needed to pay off the phone. I called the next day to find out why my sons phone still had no service with the new SIM card. This time when I called I had a young lady on the phone and she had trouble answering my questions and basically repeated herself for at least 10 minutes just repeating everything I said back to me and then finally told me that she cannot unlock the phone until my entire bill is paid in full! Are you kidding me???!!! I spent all that time on the phone with her, not to mention all the time I was put on hold for her to “go and look into the matter”! She ultimately could not help me but instead wasted my time!! I do not recommend them at all and not just for this reason but also because the cell coverage is horrible!!!

Horrible customer service! 
They added a "free line" to my account and then charged me for it!  Took HOURS on the phone to get them to understand. They kept saying it was free. I said "but you are charging me for it". Finally after speaking with several people, I got the line and the charges removed. BUT IT TOOK HOURS.

I then bought a new phone from T-moble because my screen was cracked, but can't activate it online because I am still registered as a Sprint customer and the device is at T-moble. but if I try to activate at t-mobile it sends me back to Sprint! SERIOUSLY??!!

It takes forever to get any phone help.  They do offer to call you back so you don't have to wait. However, when I have accepted a call-back. my phone rings and then they keep me on hold. Three times I have waited up to 30 minutes AFTER being called back! Finally (assuming I was lost in the system) I have hung up.

Several of the support people have barely understandable English (and I am super tolerant of accents), or phones with static or seem to be calling from a kennel full of barking dogs. They are scraping the bottom of the barrel.

Supposedly everything is working now but my experience with T-mobile since the Sprint merger leaves me with ZERO confidence that this is going to work. If things don't get resolved I plan to throw in the towel and go with Mint.  

If I must endure substandard service, at least I can pay a substandard price.

Mint might even provide better service. T-mobile has lowered the customer service bar to zero

So. Don't believe u get 20 percent off if u change to tmobile from boost. I was told in store that my bill would be 56.00 after a 20 percent off. Well... get a text the bill is 70.00. Called customer service and they tell me the plan I am on is not covered with the 20 percent off deal.  Even though people at store told me it was. Been with tmobile for 1 week. Already been lied to and no offer of making it right. Just watch for what they tell u because it is probably wrong.

I ordered a new phone from T-Mobile and 4 times the delivery date was changed.  The last time I was given a delivery date of 28/SEP/2021.  I told T-Mobile that if the phone was not sent on this date I would cancel my order.

  Well today was the day for delivery and no phone.  I called 3 times today to cancel my order.  I said I wanted to talk to a manager and while waiting on hold my call was dropped.  The second time I  was told the manager was busy.  When I said I  would wait and was hung up on.  The 3rd time, I  called back and was put on hold for 41 minutes with no one giving me updates.  I finally had to hang up.  I have been with T-Mobile for almost 20 years, but it is time for a change.  I would highly recommend if someone is thinking about using T-MOBILE look at other carriers as well.  Managerial staff does not care about customer satisfaction and yes I am still trying to cancel my order.

T-Mobile is by far the worst not just mobile company any company that I've ever had the displeasure of having to speak with. I got a brand new phone from T-Mobile and switched over to T-Mobile 2 months ago. 5 days ago my phone stopped working properly 95% of the times my calls wouldn't go through people couldn't call me I couldn't text or they couldn't text me. Since then it has started to be a nightmare. I in total in the last 3 days have spoken to nine customer service reps three supervisors three advanced technical support people and my phone is still not working I've spent a total of 11 hours on the phone with these people on my days off and they think that this is acceptable. They sent me to a T-Mobile store to get a new SIM card this was their newest idea of what was wrong with my phone. I made sure I asked the customer service rep that they weren't going to charge me to give me a new SIM card she said absolutely not it's our policy to replace your sim card inserted in your phone and make sure all your systems are working before you leave the store. So I spent more of my own time going to the T-Mobile store they told me to go to I approached the guy at the desk explain the situation he said absolutely not with an attitude they're not right. I will not give you a SIM card I have eight SIM cards left and they are for my new customers who are having their phones turned on. When I asked him what I was supposed to do next he said I am not technical support so I don't know what to tell you. I said so you don't hold up to what your company says that they are going to do for paying customers. He said to me with a nasty attitude you know what you have a good day and walked away from me. I proceeded to finally after restarting my phone starting at restarting it starting it restarting it was able to get a hold of customer service again for the 11th time she said I will have my supervisor call you immediately guess what I heard from nobody. So they just think I'm going to go away I am not I call them again this time the customer service rep said I'm going to transfer you over to my supervisor. Then she comes back and says my supervisor said he will call you back I said no absolutely not the last time I heard that I never got a call back so I'm 11 hours into this anyways I got all night I'll wait. She put me on hold again she comes back and says her supervisor said that if I want to speak to him he will call me back and that's not negotiable. Guess what I have not heard from him. This is by far nothing I have ever experienced in my life with any company the service and the customer service and the managers are 100 times better at McDonald's and their teenagers then what T-Mobile is. They are rip off their service doesn't work and they treat their customers like crap and they don't care that you go without a phone
I can't tell you how many times a representative asked me oh do you have another phone that you can call me on how many people nowadays have a landline I mean it was just ridiculous ridiculous and disrespectful over and over again and I still have no resolution to this and to top things off after I took more time to go to the T-Mobile store that they told me to go to and the guy was rude and said they're not giving me a SIM card when I spoke to that customer service representative sitting in the parking lot of that store she had the nerve to say to me well can you just go to another store. I'm telling you do not I repeat I do not ever ever use T-Mobile their services terrible their customer service their technical support and especially their supervisors are downright rude disrespectful and do not care about the customer who's paying their hard-earned money and not getting what they are paying for. So it ended today basically by them telling me sorry but we can't do anything for you. The thing is when people try to screw me over I get relentless I'm going to be speaking to these people more than they speak to their family I am not going away I don't care how much time I got to take I will be calling them every hour on the hour . I don't do well with disrespectful rude entitled people and companies who don't care about their customers. Again I repeat if you want to save your sanity save your money and actually have a service that works do not use T-Mobile.
Completely incompetent people in customer service. I keep getting "promised" and "guaranteed" that they will fix my issue.  Totally useless and a HUGE waste of time

I signed up for the McAfee monitoring because of the TMobile data breach.  Received notice that my phone# and DL# were compromised.  Called McAfee and was told someone would call me back at a later date.  Called TMobile and was on hold.  Call was finally answered only to be hung up on when I stated the problem.  Tried to call back and was on hold for almost an hour and I finally hung up.  Seems like TMobile is all about the money and doesn't care about customer service.  If there is a big problem, it will only be multiplied by the amount of time they keep you waiting with no resolution.
Worst carrier out there. 5 g should not mean no service. Sim card issues big time. I think I am on my 6th one. Absolutely terrible.

I had Sprint since 1999 then when they merged with T-Mobile my service was aweful, calls kept cutting out, dropped calls and many times I couldn't call out and I work and live by the water in Michigan.  I spent several MONTHS and HOURS in hopes it would get fixed, went to store to change SIMM card, tech support several times, they claimed they had someone come out to "check the tower" which was false as I run into a lot of people at our marina who cannot even call out with T-Mobile service.  I filed a report with the FCC, no help besides getting a call from Sprint/T-Mobile corporate that gave no compassion for how many issues I had then charged me an early termination on my lease, real classy right?  I shouldn't have had to pay anything for no service!!!  I hope they go out of business soon after shifting all of their customer service calls to the Philippines and those people are not properly trained.  I highly recommend getting Verizon for service and have had no issues since I switched to them.  I was totally embarrassed trying to talk to my clients on my Sprint phone and still in thought of suing for lost wages.

Horrible- customer service never helps. Their plans do not work in San Francisco or Atlanta, two major metropolitan areas. I never get service or internet and every time I call they either don't pick up or tell me to shut my phone off.

I have been a T-Mobile customer for three years now since 2018, and have had conflictions and complications that have influenced my decision to cut ties with them. 

First, the slow 5G network...I bought an iPhone 12 a year ago soon after the new Apple phone release, and paid at least $950 for the phone and the "luxury" of having 5G network. I expect to get my money's worth if I am paying close to a thousand dollars, but instead I paid for extremely slow, snail speed network that inconveniently does not work at a necessary time. 

I have also experienced lagging and faulty phone service. Most days I am not able to receive service in a chunk of locations, most importantly including my job. Customers and guests at my workplace have had multiple instances involving issues connecting to our store's WiFi or receiving signal. I had asked these customers their cell phone service carrier.... it was T-Mobile and had claimed they wanted to cancel their service as well! Now I know I am not the only one.....GOODBYE T-Mobile!!!!
Rude tech support, was very unhelpful and unprofessional

If possible I would give NEGATIVE 5 stars, I was lied repeatedly by TMobile I was sold a watch during promotion for a free watch however I never got any promotional offer honored I had to pay full price for a crap watch which was suppose to be free during their promotion when I contacted TMOBILE their support managers were all ride and simply told me to SHUT The F off shame on TMobile they are truly cheaters..

DO NOT USE FOR TRACKING DEVICES! They came out to our business and gave a presentation only to not provide.  They partnered with GEOTAB and there is huge disconnect in communication between the two.  The devices don't work and they can't figure out to correct the problem but still want to charge us.  Horrible customer service and tech support.  Between the bill and having them installed on our vehicles we're thousands of dollars in the hole with nothing to show for it.  This was a huge waste of money.

Sprint customers beware. Don't switch to T-Mobile. Serious billing issues. The Stillwater T-Mobile store will promise you a rate for your lines and then sign you up for a more expensive plan that you will not be aware of until the first billing cycle. I have spent two months attempting to resolve this rip-off with no luck. It will take longer than 30 days to resolve, so you then lose your right to return the phones and get a refund. They are experts at deception. Store Manager is worthless. T-Mobile online support is worthless. Stay with Sprint as long as you can then find another carrier.

Two hours on the phone because they sold me the wrong watch!!! Find a different company, this is a joke. 17 years with sprint for seven months with T-Mobile the worst experience I’ve ever had!!!

10-25-21
Still no return box for the watches after fighting on the phone for hours and hours with these people are $&@“ . T Mobile is the most pitiful excuse for a cellular carrier that I have ever seen. I have had the worst experience over the last 7 months between their nonexistent cell service, constant excuses about towers being worked on, and their incompetent customer service. I can’t even stay on hold long enough to speak to a customer service representative because my calls drop. I have to pull over because I don’t even have enough service for my phone GPS to work. I have been told tickets were being entered for my issues that never were. I am paying for a phone that I haven’t been able to use because they claim to be working on the towers… for 7 months and counting. Do not waste your time or money on this service. T mobile the biggest thieves out there, you wanna loose money, joint them, you wanna throw your time and money away? joint them. would you like to have the worst experience in your entire live... joint them take one of their plans and you'll be next victim. the customer service is the worse in the entire nation, these guys all they care is to take your money and just laugh on how they rip off your money.  go to the stores and they will offer you nothing but lies when your bill comes up..... BOOMMM!!!! they got you taking the money you didnt agree on, then they will tell you they refund the money to check with your bank, that is just to get you off the line so you call again and have to explain everything to another agent that will tell you the same....  and then you speak with so called managers and they will fill you in trying to convince you they care and nothing gets done....

I tried to use their website but first off it doesn't work. When it finally starts working after trying for an hour you can't find anywhere to discuss an overcharge in your bill. I'm now in a contract an will lose money if I cancel so I'm screwed! I HATE TMOBILE !!!! I would tell anyone their customer service SUCKS.

I got a T-mobile magenta plan with a $830 promotional offer, i trade in 3 devices for 3 new iphone 12. I would expect to get 3* $830 = $2490 dollars in credit but in only received $830 + $157.I had to call the customer care to make them understand, each customer rep promises but nothing really happens. 

For a single thing to happen, i would have to keep calling customer care.

Secondly, i wanted to add a new line but i couldnt. Why?. i dont have credit approved it seems. Different customer reps promised that this will get fixed but its been more than a month but nothing happened.

Frustrated. Very Frustated!

Trying to get into contact with customer service is a joke! The sales associates are awful. Guy opens 2 freaking accounts in my name and now I have a balance that I’m “suppose” to pay for a account that I DID NOT MAKE!!!! F this company!

I needed a case and new glass protector on my phone.  I dread this kind of errand.  My past experience with another company, was aggravating because of the attitude and lack of help. Today I went to the T-Mobile on Main St. in North Tewksbury and had an entirely different experience.  Armando was GREAT!  I left there very happy.  Next time I will not dread the errand at all.  Thank you Armando!

Having trouble with reception on my A32.  I went into a T Mobile store, and a young man named Eli helped me.  As soon as I walked into the store, Eli acknowledged me, and said he would be with me in a moment (normally, I am ignored).  Eli was very kind and helpful to me, and the next customers who came in after me, giving them information quickly to help.
T Mobile is lucky to have someone like Eli on their team.

I've been with Sprint for years and have a great plan that T-Mobile is not going to honor.  For their most basic plan, my rate will more than double.  Got an email for a free I-phone 12.  Went to the T-mobile store and specifically said I did not want to migrate.  They said I was entitled to an upgrade for a free I-phone 12.  So, why not do it?  Well, it's not free.  I'm being charged $17 per month for the phone.  My old phone was paid for and worked perfectly fine.  I only did it because it was supposed to be free.  Customer service says there is nothing they can do.  Will not be migrating to T-mobile!  Horrible, horrible company.

We were Sprint customers for many years and NEVER had any issues at all.  We did not ask to be transferred to T-Mobile but we were and everything went south from that point on.  I am a small business owner and I noticed that I was not receiving any voice messages or text messages which was very odd.  So, I contacted our local T-Mobile office and was told that I needed a T-Mobile SIM card which was never provided to me.  With the installation of the new SIM card our phones went to "No Service" unless I stood in the middle of our driveway.  The T-Mobile rep said he could put my Sprint SIM card back into our phones if we wanted and I said then I will go back to no voice and text messages.  He agreed.  The next day, I went to the T-Mobile store, again, and talked with a different rep who showed me a map our the T-Mobile service in the area where we live and it was the "poorest reception area" on the map.  She recommended we switch to a different phone carrier.  BEST advice yet!!  We switched to Verizon and everything seems to be back to NORMAL again.

I visited the Rockledge, FL T-Mobile store to get a hotspot and data plan. They claimed they couldn't get what I wanted within 3 days, sold me a lesser unit. The whole visit was delayed by a "new" employee who constantly interrupted the one helping me. Took over 20  minutes. A nearby store got me what I wanted, delivered in 1 day, and it took 5 minutes.

I have been with Sprint since 2014. I recently had to switch to T-mobile. Once I switched my sim card I no longer had 5g coverage even though I am in the middle of 5g coverage map. Do not use the company. Customer service is terrible

This has been the most time consuming phone customer service I’ve ever had! They promised us a great business plan! Cut service numerous times on auto pay & still not right on billing ,phones & credit! Going on for months ! I will Not use this service & definitely would not recommend them!

My wife and I went to T-mobile in S-Tulsa 103rd & Memorial 9/25/21 just looking for phones.   Both of our phones have to be ordered.   No problem.  But the associates insist on putting new SIM cards in our current phones today.   Said that is what we have to do to get the discount on the phone.   I prefer to purchase my phones outright which is what I did with Sprint for 25 years.     
        What they DIDN'T TAKE INTO ACCOUNT that our current Sprint account is a BUSINESS account.   This is a Saturday and my wife (Realtor) has 2-3 deals going right now and I as a Handyman was working on a deal just one hour before they BRICK our phones!
        They said OH, this is a business phone, this can take longer and we both with our businesses have our phones bricked and no calls, no text messages which is HOW WE DO BUSINESS.    This is our income maker! 
       They hand us our phones and say they are sorry there is nothing they can do.  I ask them for a loaner phone or something since they just screwed up our phones so we can do business!   Sorry.    I said what about a trac phone I mean do something to help your customer.      T
     They tell us it could take up to 24 hours for our phones to activate.   My wife just got off the phone with a mobile rep and they told her it could take up to 72 hours!!!!!!!!!!!  CRAP !!!!!!
       This company sucks and we will cancel both of our back ordered phones and cancel this contract.  
       I don't care if they have a good network or not.   
SPRINT NEVER treated me like this!!!!!!!!!!!!!
Tmobile you SUCK!   Look elsewhere My first hour with Tmobile is the worst service I've ever received.
      Plus a rude employee Tyler was sitting on a table and lifted a butt cheek and farted right in front of my wife.   Arrogant kid.   
      Plus they couldn't get my sim card out and sent me to a phone Dr costing me another $30 offering no compensation.
WHAT A DIFFERENCE A DAY MAKES
One Day later 9/26/21.   We went to the T-mobile store @ 6900 and some odd S Lewis in Tulsa that used to be my Sprint store.   Sedrick listened to our above story and immediately was shocked at our treatment and looked at our account.   He got on the phone with someone who had the ability to port our numbers over and get the account migrated to the TMobile system.   This took about an hour and he stayed on the phone until he got our problem resolved.    THIS is what I call customer service and on a Sunday!!!   They were polite and professional and we did learn that the stores are franchised and the 2 stores we dealt with were with different owners/franchises.     Good Lord, if you have to deal with Tmobile in Tulsa, Oklahoma go to the Store on the NE corner of 71st and Lewis just north of the Mazzio's pizza.    Sedrick restored my faith in THIS Tmobile Franchise and I would give them a 12 out of 10 and the 103rd and Memorial store would get a -3 on a 1-10 scale.    The 71st and Lewis store has knowledge!!!!!      
      Just wanted to follow up and let any former Spring users wanting to switch to Tmobile should go to the 71st and Lewis store.   Top notch and they didn't say anything bad about the other franchise.  Very professional.

3 DAYS AGO WENT TO VERIZON!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!
Now I  have zero service because they had me factory reset and I have no LTE service for 3 days and miss calls from my wife and probably clients too.   Missing business.   I can't browse the web, make phone calls unless I am home with wifi and even then can't browse the web or download apps.

I MISS MY SPRINT!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

Top 2 Verizon and Tmobile can't give me service!

Waited 2 hours for a call back for a simple question. Then they finally called me back...

And put me on hold.
Are you kidding me??
Screw you. I'm done with this crappy carrier.

Haven’t had T-Mobile in bout 10-15 years but on September 22 2021 I ordered one of their HOTSPOTS devices I did all that was ask of me including paying them but what I don’t understand it’s the 25th and I was told I would get my package on the 24th now I’m not mad it was delayed I’m mad because I been getting the run around since the 22th and now I finally got the email with my information to check in my order I was told by the 1st lady it was delayed because I didn’t sign my documents when I know I did because the young men that did my order walked me though it so that right there made me hella mad that u have the nerve to lie to your customers then I called right back because I knew it was a lie and this lady said it was because they was out of stock now I don’t know why the 1st lady lied because at this point I don’t trust nothing that come from this company and for y’all to be T-Mobile this is what y’all call center do LIE smh this make no sense at all if I didn’t need my hotspot I would for show get my money back never in my life I’m sorry but y’all need to do better yall been around to long to treat y’all customers this way.
For such a large company, they have no customer service support for customers. Although they expect you to be prompt with your payment, they are very slow to help you when you need support.

Tried unlocking a phone, was on hold for an hour then got hung up on, called again waited another hour when he said the wait time would be 2-5 minutes, found a different number online for customer service and the agent started asking weird questions, when I asked why it was relevant he got aggressive, going to t-mobile in person to try and get real help but it all felt like a scam.

whatever you do, don't try to have a phone unlocked. The amount of runaround I've gotten just to unlock my dang phone for my aunt has been unreal. The first guy supposedly did it for me - my aunt goes to switch her phone and it wasn't unlocked. I called again the lady said I needed a number off the phone - yet the first guy didn't need a number off the phone. Ok, I get the number from my aunt - I call back and after an hour wait I get somebody and the girl tells me she can't find me in the system at all. Even though the other two people could find me. I ask her to transfer me to somebody else who can help me. That guy easily finds my account but then tells me I am talking to the pre paid unlock group and I need the post paid unlock group to unlock my phone. I am now on hold for that person. I've now been on the phone tonight with Tmobile for 2 hours.. They clearly don't want to unlock this phone for my poor aunt who is on a fixed income and on social security.. I gave this company a crap ton of money and they can't do one of the easiest things in the world for me..

After 8hr customer service struggle and long wait to replace lost old phone, After call center scam and lies, I am done. 
T-Mobile does value 7year customer loyalty! Tmobile had me pay for Assurant warranty for five years, then expect me to pay additional  $149 to replace said phone! It is a scam because similar phone is on the market for $189.

For the rest of the day and evening, T-Mobile customer service would not take my complaint calls. Be ware of TMobile scam call center customer service!!!

so i left i went to verizon and quickly realized they were a rip off and tried to charge me way too much but they had great service

Horrible network. Poor data service. It sucks when you have to wait couple minutes to open, GPS, YouTube or any web pages. Sometimes you can't. I won't recommend anyone to use this horrible network.
Terrible place , I am not even going to waste my time going in to detail about these disrespectful people, at least I know not to deal with this crap company.

Honestly, I am really surprised at the low scores of these consumers, because I cannot say enough about how well T-Mobile has really take care of me, specifically Phil ID#2196859.  In fact this man single handedly, made me have a complete different view of cellphone providers, that I have personally encountered in my 42 years. Most companies, are out to dupe their customers into hidden fees, excessive charges that were not there during sign up and once the get you in, you are trapped in the endless cycle of more and more fees and customer service calls. Well, this employee, Phil ID#2196859, single handedly change the game of cellphone customer service providers. 

I am a semi new customer, for me it is because I usually pick a company, and stick with them, as long as they are treating me like a valuable piece to a successful business.  This is any business, multi billion dollar business, to the local mom and pop business that has been open since the beginning of time. Customer service is the road for businesses to connect with their customers, customer service should be looked at as a reflection of how a businesses history is with their customers, but I want to say, that sometimes that reflection is fabricated. 

I had had issues with my phone, it's a new phone and I have already had to replace it three times, it's not a top tier cellphone but I don't mind all that, bells and whistles are not my thing. Function and Accessibility is all I care about. But I need my cellphone to work, I use my phone for work and this phone I got at sign up, crashes every two seconds, so my original complaint was only directed to the phone not my service. I actually had a semi hard time trying to trade my original phone back or have it replaced even though I had that specific phone for two days literally and not exactly two, it was more like 36 hours. But I requested to speak to a supervisor and she apologized for the inconvenience of the previous specialist behavior that his behavior was not what T-Mobile stands for.  She was very kind, and seemed to have fix everything, I had a new phone arriving, with a return label for the the broken one. 

I got my new bill, and not only was the charges that has previously been credited was back. She had not done what she had said she would do.  But I am a very fair person, I wasn't angry at all. I didn't know what had happened, it could have very well be within the system but it was my bottom line. My decision to call T-Mobile was ultimately to part ways or at least find out what I need to do to close my account, even if there was a charge.  But T-Mobile must have felt my vibe coming through the phone and sent in the he bug guns, because this is where Phil stepped in.  

I wanna also say, I am writing this review, on my experience with them, because of this particular person. Everything that is stated here is 100% truth, I find it unethical to write reviews that are fabricated for an incentive for that person or company. And I kind of feel like the sheer amount of bad reviews isn't and shouldn't reflect your decisions about T-Mobile, they make it extremely easy, and they also give some bad credit a chance without exuberant price fluctuations. T-Mobile in my opinion is highly underrated and I also believe majority of these reviews are baseless and not accurate. 

When I got Phil on the line it truly was a huge blessing for me and my family. As I have been dealing with these cellphones for a couple months and to be honest I don't have the energy.  My husband is in ICU and my brother just passed from Covid. And I don't think that I would be a customer if it weren't for Phil.  He was genuinely a very kind person, he should amazing customer service skills, had a clear understanding of his job, and executed a job with such professionalism, and care that I felt so grateful that I came directly here to make sure he gets recognized and If I could speak to his supervisor, I would say.. if you had about 3000 Phil's, within this company they would have five stars everywhere, the supervisor that did not do what she she promised, was no where near a professional as he was. And I honestly from the bottom of my heart, and a lifetime customer because of Phil. I cannot stress enough, him being a kind person was so important in this situation. nd he did just that and I can't tell you enough how he made my day turn completely around, I am truly grateful to this gentleman and if you are reading this Phil, thank you again!

Companies like T-mobile should be just closed but this is USA a country  where everybody tries to robber everybody else. The problem is if we move to other company, they will do the same...so what? This is USA, a country that everybody is allowed to robber everybody else. W t f!!!

The worst customer service.  Just like every other cell phone, cable service.  They promise everything up front, but when the bill comes in or when you need something later, they do NOT stand behind what they say!!  Always the same thing, "well we cannot do that and that employee(s) should not have authorized it".  By then it's too late and you are stuck with a phone and service that SUX!  The last straw is when Christine B., employee number 488686, a "supervisor", told me she would try and rectify my issue, but PROMISED to call me back either way today.  It's 6p and still no call.  Not surprised though.  I'll go back to Verizon before I sign back up with this service.

T-Mobile is a rip off. I’ve had trouble with them for years, but I’m really angry right now because somebody talked me into getting an iPhone 11 and I’m not happy with it at all and my payments have nearly doubled. I was originally told My payments would be a specific amount and T-Mobile nearly doubled that with this iPhone.  So I called T-Mobile to ask if I can return the phone because I couldn’t afford it, but they said I had it too long. I am disabled and I have brain damage and physical issues and I didn’t realize it how long it wasThat I had this phone. Now I’m making payments on the phone I can’t afford and I don’t have food in my refrigerator because of it. T-Mobile bites.

First they get hacked very seriously and I know I’ve been hacked to because my phone and my Wi-Fi have been acting up ever since the hack began and I feel like sometimes somebody’s actually watching me so I block off my camera for safety reasons. I’m very disappointing with T-Mobile I don’t know how they can say that they’re the best coverage of the country when I think there are many other companies which are much better and don’t give you so much difficulties. Not happy at all.

Beware T mobile 
Folks do not keep any direct payment arrangement with T Mobile. They have taken $900/- from my bank account when I have signed up only for my monthly payments without giving any information via text. I feel betrayed by T mobile.
Here is my story. - I had a phone with insurance which needs to be replaced. They sent me a phone and asked me to send our old one back. I have sent the old phone via their waybill number .after a month they have taken full phone money of $680/- telling they didn’t receive the phone . 
By enquiring came to know via their own associates that their system had a wrong serial number which they never receive in their life’s time. So the agent said it’s a mistake and escalated to refund it to me. 

Months passed nothing came. When I called for refund they said they didn’t exchange the phone with insurance and my phone has a liquid damage so I have to pay the money . 

They didn’t send me any information so far regarding the picture of liquid damage  and no communication or explanation about the phone is not gone through insurance ( paid $20/ every month fir insurance ) and why they have taken the full phone money from my bank account.
I was given a concession of $250/- after calling 20 times from my end .

I feel betrayed and cheated by Tmobile. 
I wanted to move from this company so as a first step I have disconnected one of my lines. 

Beware of Tmobile .

Has been an awful experience! I had to place my sister in a nursing home and switch her to my carrier.  We thought this tasks completed after many hours of conversation and labor on our part we found out she in fact was never cancelled and they lost my POA paper work. So we are back to the start  I can't express how frustrating this has been.  AND NOT OVER YET!  charge 74.00 and 70.00  And frankly expensive in attention to outrageously bad service!!!

i used to recommend tmobile to my friends and family -never again.  The customer service is horrific and they cancelled my insurance by accident and then i had to resolve - oh and go to zagg cuz their screen protection insurance is horrific as wll

I have been a T Mobile customer for 8 years and until recently have been pretty happy with the service. It is horrible the automatic part is redundant asking for your phone number over and over again. I was on the phone for an hour last night and never got anyone. So I hung up

T-Mobile has consistently struggled to provide stable service in my area outside Houston. Calls to their customer service are met with multiple sorries and credits. Very few months have gone by that I did not have at least one credit on either my phone or home internet account. I do pay for this as wait times on customer service are one hour plus usually. Too bad T-Mobile is such an uncarrier. Maybe one day they will figure out how to run a cell phone business.
